title: Level 504
wikitext:
{{CSS|content= :root { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2); } .page-header, .page-side-tools, aside, .article-comments-app { filter: drop-shadow(0 0 4px #000); } .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-link-color: #003366; } .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-link-color: #003366; } }} {{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 504 - "Munitions Depot"}}
'''“Listen, I don't know what you want from me. Like I said, I was casually strolling through..."'''
'''"Of course I'm being ironic. This is the Backrooms; you think anything we do here is casual? I was halfway out of my mind when I when reached for that vibrating door."'''
'''"Will you stop interrupting? Humming, vibrating same difference. It was a green door on a white house. I pushed through and found myself in that hellscape you call Level 504.”'''

[[File:PCAPP Bunkers.jpg|thumb|521x521px|center]] {{Alt Class|ent=0|env=0|ext=4|no=504}} ==
'''Description:'''
== {| class="article-table" ! style="background-color: #caa75f" | '''Here, there is nothing elegant, nothing unique, no gimmicks nor special properties. Level 504 is as what it appears to be at the very first glance: a military depot. Row upon row of “igloo” styled, earth-covered bunkers, designed for storage of military munitions. They extend without end; they repeat without end. These igloos are secured by large steel doors, all which are unlocked if not somewhat unwieldy to maneuver. The insides are barren, dark, and musty, with an unpleasant waft of mildew lingering in the still air. With some luck, it is possible to find an old empty pallet or two, maybe an empty barrel or crate. There are no other resources to be found, otherwise.''' '''The environment is arid, with browned grass and shrubbery, moisture entirely absent from every leaf and stem. There are no sources of food or water and with the exception of a rare cricket, there are neither animal nor entity to be found either. There is a backdrop of a mountain range decorating the otherwise flat terrain, which, despite any distance traveled, is entirely unreachable. It is as though these towering slopes taunt the eyes with a false hope that witnessing a change in scenery is within the realm of possibility. Temperatures range from a scorching 110 °F during the day, then falling to a drastic, chilling 40 °F at night. A faint breeze may occasionally grace a wanderer, but due to the lack of humidity in the level, it provides little comfort.''' !style="border-left:solid 2px #d8ad26" style="background-color: #caa75f" | {{Blurimage|file=Igloo Inside.jpg|caption=The inside of a bunker.|width=337x337px}} |}
'''“I don’t know what else you want from me. Describe my feelings? Feelings… no, just desperation, loneliness. I dragged myself from bunker to bunker, hoping to find something new. My supplies ran out just after a few days and there was nothing to satisfy my hunger; nothing to quench my thirst. I caught a cricket, desperate for protein. It wasn’t enough, it was gooey… made me gag, but in the Backrooms you can’t afford the luxury of being picky.”'''
==
'''Presence of Life:'''
== '''There are no entities nor animal life present on Level 504 with the exception of the occasional cricket. Due to the lack of resources and the infrequency of encountering Level 504, no colony or outpost has ever been established. It is not recommended to waste any resources in attempts to build any sort of colony or camp in this level.''' ==
'''Entrances and Exits:'''
==
'''“I lost track of time. After countless days and nights, starving, losing my mind… I honestly stopped caring. Who knows how long I was there. Long enough to nearly die of thirst and hunger, not long enough to actually keel over; obviously. I don’t know. It was nighttime and due to dizziness, I went blind for a second. When my vision returned, I was in a valley of lotus plants next to a pink stream.”'''
'''Accounts of wanderers visiting Level 504 are far and few between. Each instance, the wanderer arrived via an exit from a level that allows passage to “any level” such as [[Level 55]] or [[Level 444]]. Exiting is rare and random as Level 504 is remarkably stable, with no method of noclipping having been discovered. Due to the very limited number of accounts from survivors of Level 504, it is presumed that most wanderers perish from starvation or dehydration. Those that do egress, will suddenly find themselves in a new place such as [[Level 503]], or [[Level 83.1]].''' ----
